title: Memory-V2V: Memory-Augmented Video-to-Video Diffusion for Consistent Multi-Turn Editing
page_evidence_status: page_numbers_available_from_extraction; table bodies require visual verification
key_takeaway: Memory-V2V formulates multi-turn video editing as a cross-turn consistency problem and treats previous edits as structured memory constraints for future generation.
key_figure_caption: Fig. 2: Memory-V2V framework with external cache retrieval, dynamic tokenization, and adaptive token merging.
claim_evidence_summary: Strong evidence for task formulation and method components; moderate-to-strong evidence for consistency gains across two tasks; table values and external publication status require verification.
transferable_insights: Treat creative history as structured constraints rather than raw context.
Use task-aware retrieval to select relevant prior states.
Evaluate process consistency separately from final visual quality.
